node_16.excerpt: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module ,
node_16.fallbackText: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module, class, or __init__ method are called “attribute docstrings”.
node_16.nodeLabel: String literals occurring immediately after simple assignment…
node_16.textQuote: String literals occurring immediately after a simple assignment at the top level of a module, class, or __init__ method are called “attribute docstrings”.

node_17.excerpt: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high - level structure of docstrings :
node_17.fallbackText: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high-level structure of docstrings: what they should contain, and how to say it (without touching on any markup syntax within docstrings). The PEP contains conventions, not laws or syntax.
node_17.nodeLabel: aim of this PEP is to standardize high-level structure of…
node_17.textQuote: The aim of this PEP is to standardize the high-level structure of docstrings: what they should contain, and how to say it (without touching on any markup syntax within docstrings).

node_18.excerpt: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “ additional docstrings ” .
node_18.fallbackText: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “additional docstrings”.
node_18.nodeLabel: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring…
node_18.textQuote: String literals occurring immediately after another docstring are called “additional docstrings”.

node_8.excerpt: “ A universal convention supplies all of maintainability , clarity , consistency , and a foundation
node_8.fallbackText: “A universal convention supplies all of maintainability, clarity, consistency, and a foundation for good programming habits too. What it doesn’t do is insist that you follow it against your will. That’s Python!”
